body.u: I sat looking out of my living room window and I could see young girls of about sixteen going past my window, the fashion has really changed from when I was that age. I am now sixty.

As I thought of this I began to think back to the days when I was that age. I had just finished my exams and I was looking around for a well paid secure job. I can remember keep going into the job centre to see if any new jobs had been put out. One day I saw one that I thought was the ideal job for me, it was well paid and it sounded quite interesting, so I rung up the personal officer and athe interview was arranged. I went for the interview and I got the job.

Once I had started the job I thought I’d be there for life. The next important thing that happened in my life was that I passed my driving test and so that meatmeant I could start looking for a new job in London or somewhere like that so that’s what I started to do. I looked at all the advertisements for jobs and flats in London.
In the end I managed to get a good well paid job and a nice cosy little flat. I used to go out about 3 or 4 nights a weeksweek with friends to a disco.
When I was 24 I got married and moved into a fairly big bungalow just outside London, but I still carried on in my old job in London.
After about 2 years left work to have a baby. I stayed at home to look after my child until she was 8 years old then I went out and got a job.

carried on working all the time my daughter was growing up, then it was her turn to get married. After that nothing much seemed to happen until about 2 years ago when my daughter had a little daughter of her own. Now I have her running around the house a lot and it reminds me of when my daughter was a baby.
